description_long: This object is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a solid, dark metal head. The handle is made from light-colored wood, displaying visible grain patterns and a gentle ergonomic curve for improved grip and control. The axe head is broad and rectangular, with a flat poll on one end and a sharp, straight cutting edge on the other, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks. The head is securely mounted onto the handle, with the wood extending through the eye of the blade and possibly wedged for stability. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. This tool is commonly found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance areas for cutting wood or demolition work.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a solid, rectangular dark metal head, designed for chopping or splitting tasks.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from an angled top-down view, highlighting the curved wooden handle and the broad, flat side of the dark metal head.
description_view_1: A side profile view shows the full length of the handle and the rectangular shape of the axe head, emphasizing the ergonomic curve of the handle.
description_view_2: This end-on view displays the thickness of the axe head and the circular cross-section of the handle where it enters the head.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile reveals the flat poll and the straight cutting edge of the blade, as well as the handle's curvature.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with visible grain, ergonomic shape, oval cross-section, and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Rectangular, single-edged steel blade with a straight cutting edge and a flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle passing through the eye of the blade.
